title: TubeTube: Perfectly Timed Videos for Your Journey
content: Are you ever bored and need something to do during your long ride on the DC Metro? TubeTube can help! Enter your start and stop stations, and TubeTube will calculate your trip length and recommend interesting videos that fit your travel time! This is a Ruby on Rails application, which interacts with both the WMATA (DC Metro/Subway) and Youtube APIs, utilizing the Figaro and HTTParty Ruby Gems. Try it out here, or view the Github repo here.

title: Calculator Race
content: This is simple game I built using Meteor JS. Two players can compete against one another to answer math questions the fastest. I used Meteor's MongoDB and Spacebars templating, as well as JQuery and Underscore JS for this project. Click here to view the project.

title: Head to Head! Game
content: The first portfolio project for my Web Development Immersive course. It's a guessing game where the players try to guess their friends preferences on random topics. I built the game logic with Ruby and routed actions using the Ruby Gem/framework, Sinatra on top of a Postgresql database. I also used Git and Github for version control and deployed to Heroku. You can see the game in action here, or view the Github repo here.

title: Drizzly Weather App
content: Collaborative project built in about 10 hours with a team of five people. This is a weather app that queries the Weather Underground API and makes dynamic AJAX calls to present conditions in the user's favorite locations. We used Rails and the Ruby Gem Devise to set up user authentication and basic HTTP routes. I contributed to setting up models, the Ruby Devise Gem, and decoding the API's data response into user-friendly views. You can view the Github repo here.
